text.title: Security by Design Feature Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Security by Design Feature Checklist` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST cover all six STRIDE categories — Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information disclosure, DoS, Elevation. Skipping a category = INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Skipped categories become incidents in the skipped category.

rule.statement: Each prompt under each category MUST resolve to one of: `mitigated | accepted | deferred | not-applicable`. 'Maybe' and 'TODO' are rejected.
Output `feature-stm.md` is ≤ 1 page (≈ 60 lines markdown). Longer = use the full STRIDE template instead.
rule.rationale: Multi-page lite checklists are not lite.

rule.statement: feature-stm.md MUST be linked from the feature's design doc, not committed in isolation.
rule.rationale: Orphan security docs are unread security docs.

rule.statement: A STRIDE-aware reviewer signs the feature-stm.md before architecture-review approval.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
